CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-09-2019 Off Calendar

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-23-2019 Continued To Sep-24-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
MINI MINUTES:
Bobby C. Walker appearing for Plaintiff. The CRC 3.1385 Order to Show Cause is re-set to Sep-24-2019 at 10:30 AM in Department 610. Judge: ANDREW Y.S. CHENG; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Proceedings not reported. (610)

Motion For Attorney Fees, Costs And Expenses
Off Calendar Jun-04-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jacob Cutler, Esq. 310-929-4900 of Strategic Legal Practices for Plaintiff through Court Call.
Nejla Nassirian, Esq. 916-780-4050 of Universal & Shannon LLP for Defendant FCS US through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff clarifies billing of fee motion. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF CARLETTE SMITH's Motion For Attorney Fees, Costs And Expenses.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Plaintiff Carlette Smith's motion for attorney fees, costs, and expenses is granted in the amount of $8,242.00 in attorneys' fees and $1,472.05 in costs and expenses. In Defendants' January 18, 2018 Code of Civil Procedure section 998 offer, which Plaintiff accepted, Defendants offered "to pay reasonable costs, expenses and attorney's fees based on actual time expended pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure Section 1794(b) as stipulated by the parties or, if the parties cannot agree, upon motion to the court having jurisdiction over this action." Despite the emphasized language, Plaintiff never made any effort to stipulate to an award of reasonable attorneys' fees, costs and expenses. Further, Plaintiff's more than one-year delay in addressing the fee issues required its counsel to travel to San Francisco to attend two order to show cause hearings and file this motion, all of which could have been avoided, had Plaintiff timely sought to resolve its fee request. Under the circumstances, substantially all of the time spent on the case after the settlement documentation was finalized on September 25, 2018 was unnecessarily and unreasonably incurred, and the Court will not award fees or costs for that time (other than for the 12/18/18 and 1/17/19 time entries, which reflect work in connection with the settlement). The Court declines to award a multiplier, as this case did not present novel or difficult questions of law or fact, the facts were not significantly disputed, and little discovery was required. Plaintiff's counsel expended only slightly more than 20 hours of work on this entire action before accepting Defendants' Section 998 offer and finalizing the settlement agreement. Under the circumstances, the contingent nature of the fee award is far outweighed by the other factors, particularly given Plaintiff's lengthy delay. (See, e.g., Hall v. FCA US LLC (E.D. Cal. May 21, 2018) 2018 WL 2298431 at *8 [declining on similar facts to award multiplier].)(as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Defendant to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Ruth Ekhaus, CSR#12219, rekhaus@sftc.org, Reported. =(302/EPS)
